title: Iran Hit a Cargo Ship in the Strait of Hormuz Days After Signing Peace
script: Iran called this peace? Iran's Revolutionary Guards struck the Singapore-flagged container ship Ever Lovely with a drone in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday. The ship's bridge was damaged. Nobody was killed. Here's the catch — just last week, Iran and the United States signed a 60-day peace roadmap, with Iran agreeing to let ships pass through the strait freely. Then Iran's military launched a drone at one. Oil prices spiked immediately. The UN's International Maritime Organization paused its evacuation of vessels stranded in the Gulf. Iran's military called it a warning. Iran's foreign ministry said shipping was fine. The two contradicted each other within hours. Is the Strait of Hormuz actually safe to transit right now?
